tags: texas department of licensing and regulation
description: List of winner claims for Texas Lottery® prizes with player (excluding identity of those designated anonymous), prize, game and selling retailer information. GOV'T § 466.411: A prize winner, or owner of a legal entity that is a prize winner, of a lottery prize in an amount equal to $1 million or more may choose to remain anonymous. If the prize is an annuity, then the prize winner may be anonymous for 30 days after the prize is claimed.

description: This report lists current calendar year Texas plant closure and layoff notices issued under the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act. Under certain circumstances, the WARN Act requires employers to provide notice 60 days in advance of plant closures or mass layoffs. The WARN Act is intended to offer protection to workers, their families and communities.

description: The Texas Department of Insurance, Division of Workers’ Compensation (DWC) publishes a quarterly report of employers with active Texas workers’ compensation insurance coverage. Employers with coverage are called “subscribers.” Texas does not require most private employers to have workers' compensation insurance coverage. Insurance carriers report coverage data to DWC using the International Association of Industrial Accident Boards and Commissions’ (IAIABC) IAIABC Proof of Coverage (POC) Release

name: Superintendent Salary Report by District for School Year 2024-2025
provider: Research and Analysis Division at Texas Education Agency

columns.name: District Name
columns.description: Identifies the name of the school district as registered with the Texas Education Agency.

columns.name: District
columns.description: The six-digit number associated with the district name registered with the Texas Education Agency. (Note: there are several districts that have the same name, but each district has an individual district number.)

columns.name: Superintendent
columns.description: Identifies the person’s first, middle, and last legal name.

columns.name: Base Pay
columns.description: The total payroll amount where payroll activity code is 80. (Does not include supplemental pay.) Please note that the amount is shown as 0 when it was not submitted by the district, including the cases where the superintendents are contracted staff members hired through a private company, agency, or direct contract.

columns.name: FTE Pay
columns.description: The calculated base pay the person would make if working in the position full-time. The formula used is base pay divided by full-time equivalent. Please note that the amount is shown as 0 when it was not submitted by the district, including the cases where the superintendents are contracted staff members hired through a private company, agency, or direct contract.

columns.name: Enrollment
columns.description: The total enrollment of the school district, based on the PEIMS Fall Snapshot.

columns.name: FTE
columns.description: The measure of the extent to which a person occupies a full-time position.  If the number of days employed is greater than or equal to 187, the FTE is a decimal number created by taking the percent of the day employed divided by 100.  If the number of days employed is less than 187, the FTE is a decimal number created by the following: (percent day employed divided by 100) x (number of days employed)/187.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The State of Texas

columns.name: Charter Status
columns.description: Identifies whether the district is an independent school district or an open enrollment charter school district.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: The Regional Education Service Center to which the school district belongs.

columns.name: County
columns.description: The county in which the district is located.

description: Texas public school district superintendent salary information as of October 2024.

description: The Texas Department of Insurance, Division of Workers’ Compensation (DWC) publishes a monthly report of private employers that have opted out of Texas workers’ compensation insurance coverage (non-subscribers). Texas does not require most private employers to have workers' compensation insurance coverage. Non-subscribers are required to file the DWC Form-005, Employers' Notice of No Coverage or Termination of Coverage annually. The DWC Form-005 is the source of this data set.

description: ► The Texas Department of Insurance (TDI) handles complaints against people and organizations licensed by TDI, such as companies, agents, and adjusters. To learn more, go to the TDI webpage, How to get help with an insurance issue or file a complaint.&nbsp ► This dataset does not include the person or organization the complaint is filed against. To view the dataset that includes the "Complaint filed against" column, use TDI Complaints: All Data.

columns.name: Radioactive Flag

columns.description: North American Industry Classification System. A code that best depicts a site's primary business activity. Only provided for hazardous wastes.

columns.description: EPA defined value for recognizing the management method of on-site treatment of waste. Also known as a system type code.

columns.description: Unique identifier assigned to registered solid waste entities.

columns.name: Origin Code
columns.description: Categorizes industrial and hazardous waste based on its source.

columns.description: Generator’s description of the waste.

columns.name: New Chemical Substance Flag
columns.description: Indicates if the waste is a new chemical substance not currently listed on the Toxic Substances Control Act Chemical Substance Inventory.

columns.name: Off Site Flag
columns.description: Indicates if the generator solely sends the waste off-site for treatment or disposal and does not manage the waste on-site.

columns.name: Texas Waste Code
columns.description: Eight-character code that consists of a four-digit sequence number, a three-digit form code, and a character for the waste classification (H, 1, 2, or 3), in that order.

columns.name: EPA Hazardous Waste Number
columns.description: EPA hazardous waste number, also known as an EPA hazardous waste code, as described in 40 Code of Federal Regulations Part 261.

columns.name: EPA Waste Form Code
columns.description: EPA defined values for recognizing the form of a hazardous waste. This form code may differ from the Texas Form Code contained within the Texas Waste Code.

columns.name: Waste Code Status Change Date
columns.description: Refers to the last update date for the waste code status.

columns.name: Waste Code Status
columns.description: Indicates the status of waste codes as active or inactive.

columns.name: Source Code
columns.description: EPA defined value for recognizing the source of a hazardous waste.

description: This table lists the EPA Hazardous Waste Numbers (EHWNs) tied to each hazardous waste a site generates. The table lists the Texas Waste Code, other information about the waste, and each EHWN in a separate row.

name: Bid Tabulations

columns.name: BID SUBMIT SEQUENCE NUMBER
columns.description: The order of the bids that are submitted.

columns.name: BID RANK SEQUENCE NUMBER
columns.description: The bids are listed from the lowest bid amount to the highest bid amount, with the winning bid at the top as number one. "EE" refers to the Engineer's Estimate. "0" or "901" refer to the bid is tossed or rejected by Commision.

columns.name: BID ITEM QUANTITY
columns.description: Indicates the quantity of items to the nearest 0.001.

columns.name: ALTERNATIVE BID CODE
columns.description: Alternative bid code that can be used to bid on the project.

columns.name: PROJECT TYPE
columns.description: These are Construction, Non-Let, Special, Transfer, Maintenance and Other Project types.

columns.name: MAXIMUM NUMBER OF WORKING DAYS
columns.description: Number of day for the contract depending on WORKING DAY TYPE.

columns.name: HIGHWAY
columns.description: Represents the highway type and number.

columns.name: SPECIFICATION DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Full description for Specification Codes to identify general groups of bid items used to build a roadway or construction project.

columns.name: CONTROLLING PROJECT ID (CCSJ)
columns.description: The primary CSJ identify as the main project for the contract.

columns.name: SPEC BOOK YEAR
columns.description: Indicates the year of the Spec Book. Spec Books are release every ten years.

columns.name: PROJECT NAME
columns.description: The name of the project.

columns.name: FORCE ACCOUNT DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Full description for the Force Account (Project cost that are not associated with the actual construction of the project).

columns.name: DBE GOAL PERCENT
columns.description: Percentage of a contract's funds to be allocated to subcontractors who are designated by the department as minorities.

columns.name: MEASUREMENT UNIT
columns.description: Abbreviation of unit of work measure for the bid item.

columns.name: NBI NUMBER
columns.description: This field contains the unique National Bridge Inventory (NBI) number.

columns.name: COUNTY
columns.description: The county(s) where the project location is identified. The project can be across the counties, while it is within the same control section.

columns.name: ENGINEER'S ESTIMATE UNIT PRICE
columns.description: This indicates the Engineer's Estimated cost per unit of the bid item.

columns.name: FEDERAL PROJECT NUMBER
columns.description: Number assigned to federally funded projects for identification purposes to track federal fundings.

columns.name: LOW BIDDER FLAG
columns.description: Indicate the low bidder

columns.name: PROJECT ACTUAL LET DATE
columns.description: Indicates the date that a CSJ went though letting or date project received construction concurrent memo.

columns.name: PROJECT ESTIMATED LET DATE
columns.description: Indicates the date that a district anticipates a project going to letting or to be obligated in the case of non-let projects.

columns.name: PROJECT ID
columns.description: Project Identification number will be assigned by TxDOTCONNECT.

columns.name: SEALED ENGINEER'S ESTIMATE CONTRACT
columns.description: Indicates the sealed engineer's estimate for the entire contract.

columns.name: BID ITEM SEQUENCE NUMBER
columns.description: Indicates the order in which the bid codes are processed on the day of the letting.

columns.name: BID ITEM DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Full description for the Bid Item.

columns.name: BID CODE
columns.description: Bid Code is comprised with the first 3-4 digits of Standard Codes to identify general groups of bid items used to build a roadway or construction project. It's followed by the last 4 digits of Item Codes that describe the bid item. However, projects will have no Bid Item Code if the Category of Work is set to Force Account.

columns.name: VENDOR NAME
columns.description: The vendor name for the bid.

columns.name: A+B ENGINEER'S ESTIMATE AMOUNT
columns.description: Indicates A+B portion of the Engineer's Estimate Amount. The amount is the Road User Cost multiplied by the Max Days.

columns.name: UTILITY ID
columns.description: Indicates unique system generated ID code for the utility.

columns.name: STATE PROJECT NUMBER
columns.description: Number assigned to the State funded projects for identification purposes to track state fundings.

columns.name: BID ITEM UNIT PRICE AMOUNT
columns.description: This indicates the cost per unit of the bid item.

columns.name: SPECIFICATION CODE
columns.description: Specification Codes is the first 3-4 digits of Bid Codes to identify general groups of bid items used to build a roadway or construction project.

columns.name: SHORT DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Short description of the project.

columns.name: SEALED ENGINEER'S ESTIMATE PROJECT
columns.description: Indicates the sealed engineer's estimate for the project.

columns.name: PROJECT NUMBER
columns.description: Number assigned to the project for identification purposes to track funds based on State Project or Federal Project.

columns.name: PROJECT LIMITS TO
columns.description: Ending location of the project.

description: The Texas Department of Insurance, Division of Workers' Compensation (DWC) maintains a database of professional medical billing services (SV1). It contains charges, payments, and treatments billed on a CMS-1500 form by doctors and other health care professionals who treat injured employees, including ambulatory surgical centers, with dates of service for the last five years. name: Local Hotel Occupancy Tax Reporting 2024
description: In the early 1970s, the Texas Legislature authorized certain local governments to begin collecting a hotel occupancy tax (HOT). Almost two decades later, the Legislature offered hotel occupancy taxing authority as one of several revenue options to support sports and community venues. The tax may be levied by a city, county or a partnership between the two. description: The allocation payment detail shows the components of the total dollars returned to a local sales taxing city, county, special purpose district or transit authority by the Comptroller's office for their local sales tax collections. The components of each payment are listed by month. Allocation amounts are dependent upon the timing and accuracy of taxpayers' returns but generally represent taxes collected on sales made two months or more prior to the allocation payment date.

description: Texas State Expenditures by County shows where state dollars are spent. It lists state expenditures by agency and type of expenditure in each county. name: CPS 2.1 Removals - by Region with Child Demographics FY2016-2025
provider: TX DFPS Data and Decision Support
description: A removal occurs when CPS determines that a child cannot safely remain in their own home and that DFPS needs to seek legal custody to ensure child safety. Removals can occur in an investigation, family preservation, family substitute care or family reunification stage. Visit dfps.texas.gov for information on CPS Abuse/Neglect Investigations and all DFPS programs.

columns.name: Release of Information Code
columns.description: DN# 526 - Identifies whether the release of information related to a claim is authorized or not. A = Appropriate release of information on file at health care service provider or at a URA. I = Informed consent to release medical information for conditions or diagnosis regulated by Federal Statues. M = The provider has a limited or restricted ability to release data related to a claim. N = No, provider is not allowed to release data. O = On file at payor or at plan sponsor. Y = Yes, provider has signed statement permitting release of medical billing data related to the claim.

description: The Texas Department of Insurance, Division of Workers' Compensation (DWC) maintains a database of institutional medical billing services (SV2). It contains charges, payments, and treatments billed on a CMS-1450 form (UB-92, UB-04) by hospitals and medical facilities that treat injured employees, excluding ambulatory surgical centers, with dates of service for the last five years. For datasets going back to 2010, see institutional medical billing services (SV2) header information – historical. T

columns.name: Line Number
columns.description: The number of the line item on the bill.

columns.name: Fourth HCPCS Modifier Billed Code
columns.description: DN# 717 - The fourth two-character code identifying special circumstances related to the procedure billed.

columns.description: DN# 731 - A code indicating the general category of the first adjustment made to the dollar amount paid or credited to the line item. CO = Contractual Obligations OA = Other Adjustments PI = Payer initiated reductions PR = Patient Responsibility

columns.name: Treatment Line Authorization Number
columns.description: DN# 738 - Defaults to the Treatment Authorization Number (DN581 at the bill level) unless a data element is transmitted in this field. Y = reported, N = not reported.

columns.description: DN# 733 - The dollar amount of the second adjustment paid or credited to the line item.

columns.description: DN# 732 - A code indicating the detailed reason of the second adjustment made to the dollar amount paid or credited to the line item.

columns.name: Service Adjustment Reason Code 1
columns.description: DN# 732 - A code indicating the detailed reason of the first adjustment made to the dollar amount paid or credited to the line item.

columns.name: Revenue Paid Code
columns.description: DN# 576 - Indicates the specific cost center paid. Examples: 117 = Private room and board oncology 201 = Intensive care surgical 515 = Clinic pediatric

columns.name: First HCPCS Modifier Paid Code
columns.description: DN# 727 - The first two-character code identifying special circumstances related to the procedure paid.

columns.name: HCPCS Line Procedure Paid Code

name: RRC-Texas North Region Original Land Survey Polygons

columns.name: LEVEL3_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL3_SUR: TEXT 8 Section number. Further describes an abstracted surveyed parcel. Or, when preceded by “SUR”, a surveyed parcel further divided into numbered abstracted areas. Example: SUR 101

columns.name: SCRAP_FILE
columns.description: SCRAP_FILE: TEXT 9 Scrap or mineral file number from the GLO Abstract of Land Titles.

columns.name: LEVEL4_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL4_SUR:  TEXT 32 Sub-Survey name of the grantee when the survey is a part of a larger refined area surveyed by a common party, and is only added if it is shown on the GLO map. A scrap file number corresponding to GLO records may also appear in the field.

columns.name: the_geom
columns.description: THE_GEOM feature gives more information about the geometry. The geometry gives more information about the shape, size, relative position of figures, and the properties of space. All this data is stored in THE_GEOM.

columns.name: ABSTRACT_L
columns.description: ABSTRACT_L: TEXT 11 Abstract label. Label for the abstract number.

columns.name: ABSTRACT_N
columns.description: ABSTRACT_N: TEXT 12 Abstract number. The Anum is comprised of the county FIPS code and the abstract number. Assigned to the surveyed parcel by the General Land Office at the time of patenting. If the abstract number field contains a "?" or is blank, then no abstract number was found.

columns.name: LEVEL2_BLO
columns.description: LEVEL2_BLO: TEXT 10 Block Number. The number or letter used in description of a group of surveys identified as a Block on the GLO map. Example: 101

columns.name: LEVEL1_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL1_SUR: TEXT 32 Survey name. The name of the original grantee or the name of the company, individual or eleemosynary institution that is common among a formed group of surveys as shown on the General Land Office (GLO) county patent survey map or the GLO State Abstract of Land Titles.

description: A survey is a measurement for a parcel of land. Original surveys were performed as part of the patenting process where land was transferred from the public domain. The original patented surveys are recorded at the Texas General Land Office. The construction and placement of these survey polygons are not to be used to define or establish survey boundaries. These survey polygons are provided as a public service for informational purposes only.

columns.description: Code identifying the resolution of a fee exemption request.

columns.description: Code identifying a fee exemption request.

columns.description: Calendar year of the annual waste report. The generator generated or handled the waste on the annual waste summary report during this year.

columns.description: Method of reporting the waste either via paper or electronic (through the State of Texas Environmental Electronic Reporting System or STEERS).

columns.description: Texas waste code for the reported waste.

columns.description: Generator’s solid waste registration number.

columns.name: Quantity Generated (lbs)
columns.description: Total quantity of waste generated in pounds (lbs). Note that the generator may have reported the quantities in either tons or kilograms, but the amounts have been converted to pounds.

columns.name: Quantity Handled (lbs)
columns.description: The amount of waste managed using the method in the management code column at the facility indicated in the Facility Number or EPA ID fields. Note that the generator may have reported the quantities in either tons or kilograms, but the amounts have been converted to pounds.

columns.name: Hazardous Waste Flag
columns.description: Did the entity generate more than 1.1 tons (2200 lbs) of hazardous in any one month period of the reporting year?

columns.name: Generator Comments
columns.description: Comments from the generator about the waste.

columns.name: Acute Hazardous Waste Flag
columns.description: Did the entity generate more than 2.2 lbs of hazardous in any one month period of the reporting year?

columns.name: EPA ID
columns.description: EPA ID of the facility that handled the waste.

columns.name: No Reporting Required
columns.description: When populated with a checkmark, indicates that the generator did not need to provide an itemized report of their waste generation because they generated less than 2000 pounds of non-acute hazardous waste, 2.2 pounds of acute hazardous waste, and 2000 pounds of Class 1 Industrial waste.

columns.name: Facility Number
columns.description: Displays either the receiver’s registration number, the state code if the receiver is located outside of Texas, or the generator’s waste management unit sequence number where the waste was handled.

description: Information from the TCEQ annual waste summary report form, including generator's registration number, the year of report, waste, total quantity generated, quantity handled and how the waste was handled. name: RRC-Texas West Region Original Land Survey Polygons

columns.name: LEVEL3_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL3_SUR: TEXT 8 Section number. Further describes an abstracted surveyed parcel. Or, when preceded by “SUR”, a surveyed parcel further divided into numbered abstracted areas. Example: SUR 101 9

columns.name: SCRAP_FILE
columns.description: SCRAP_FILE: TEXT 9 Scrap or mineral file number from the GLO Abstract of Land Titles.

columns.name: the_geom
columns.description: THE_GEOM feature gives more information about the geometry. The geometry gives more information about the shape, size, relative position of figures, and the properties of space. All this data is stored in THE_GEOM.

columns.name: ABSTRACT_L
columns.description: ABSTRACT_L: TEXT 11 Abstract label. Label for the abstract number.

columns.name: LEVEL4_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL4_SUR: TEXT 32 Sub-Survey name of the grantee when the survey is a part of a larger refined area surveyed by a common party and is only added if it is shown on the GLO map. A scrap file number corresponding to GLO records may also appear in the field.

columns.name: LEVEL1_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL1_SUR: TEXT 32 Survey name. The name of the original grantee or the name of the company, individual or eleemosynary institution that is common among a formed group of surveys as shown on the General Land Office (GLO) county patent survey map or the GLO State Abstract of Land Titles.

columns.name: ABSTRACT_N
columns.description: ABSTRACT_N: TEXT 12 Abstract number. The Anum is comprised of the county FIPS code and the abstract number. Assigned to the surveyed parcel by the General Land Office at the time of patenting. If the abstract number field contains a "?" or is blank, then no abstract number was found.

columns.name: LEVEL2_BLO
columns.description: LEVEL2_BLO: TEXT 10 Block Number. The number or letter used in description of a group of surveys identified as a Block on the GLO map. Example: 101

description: A survey is a measurement for a parcel of land. Original surveys were performed as part of the patenting process where land was transferred from the public domain. The original patented surveys are recorded at the Texas General Land Office. The construction and placement of these survey polygons are not to be used to define or establish survey boundaries. These survey polygons are provided as a public service for informational purposes only.

description: An Aggregate Production Operation (APO) refers to any site where aggregates are being (or have been) removed or extracted from the earth, including the entire area of extraction, stripped areas, haulage ramps, and the land on which plant processing for the raw materials is located. Rock quarries, gravel pits, borrow pits, and other commercial APOs are required to register with TCEQ and undergo recurring inspections. APOs are included with their location, material type, permit number, and other i

columns.description: Country associated with physical licensed location address.

columns.description: Texas county associated with physical licensed location address.

columns.description: Name of the owner submitting the application for a TABC license.

columns.name: Trade Name
columns.description: Name of the establishment (often a DBA).

columns.description: Reference number associated with all licenses held by a legal entity (owner). One unique Master File ID is associated with each unique owner entity.

columns.name: Phone
columns.description: Business phone number.

columns.name: ZIP
columns.description: ZIP code associated with the physical location address (no hyphen).

columns.name: State
columns.description: State (abbreviation) associated with the physical location address.

columns.name: City
columns.description: City associated with the physical location address.

columns.name: Address 2
columns.description: Suite number or other detail further identifying the physical location address included in the application for a TABC license.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: Physical location address (street number and street name) included in the application for a TABC license.

columns.name: Gun Sign
columns.description: Displays either Blue or Red. If Red, this indicates that a business is required to post a red handgun warning sign. View TABC’s Sign Requirements webpage for more information. No action is required for the Blue designation as the blue signs are no longer required under state law.

columns.name: Wine Percent
columns.description: Indicates either 14 or 17 percent volume for applicable TABC license classes.  Authorized retailers may sell malt beverages and wine not more than 14 percent or 17 percent (depending on type of local-option election).

columns.name: Submission Date
columns.description: The date the AIMS application was submitted to TABC for consideration.

columns.name: Subordinate License ID
columns.description: Unique numerical reference to a TABC subordinate license. Maximum of nine (9) numerals. These are the new license numbers associated with the Alcohol Industry Management System (AIMS).Note:  This numerical reference will only be assigned upon approval of the application.

columns.name: Primary License ID
columns.description: Unique numerical reference to a TABC primary license. Maximum of nine (9) numerals. These are the new license numbers associated with the Alcohol Industry Management System (AIMS).Note:  This numerical reference will only be assigned upon approval of the application unless it is an existing primary license associated with a new subordinate license application.

columns.name: ApplicationStatus
columns.description: The status associated with the AIMS application as reflected in the AIMS system. Note: only those applications whose status value equal “Received” or “In Review” are included in Public Inquiry.

name: Landlord/Tenant Monthly Caseload CY 2023-2026

columns.name: Cases Pending
columns.description: All cases previously filed in which a judgment had not been entered at the beginning of the month.

columns.name: Active Cases Pending
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered that were active at the beginning of the month.

columns.name: New Cases Filed
columns.description: Total number of new cases filed during the month.

columns.name: Cases Reactivated
columns.description: Number of cases that had previously been placed in an inactive pending status, bus for which further court proceedings and activities can now be resumed so that the case can proceed to disposition.

columns.name: All Other Dispositions
columns.description: Number of dispositions not clearly identifiable as any of the above categories.

columns.name: Cases Non-Suited or Dismissed by Plaintif
columns.description: Number of cases in which a dismissal was entered at the request of the plaintiff or petitioner or dismissed by agreement of both parties.

columns.name: Cases Dismissed for Want of Prosecution
columns.description: Number of cases dismissed because the plaintiff or petitioner did not appear or otherwise made no effort to pursue their case.

columns.name: Jury Trials
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment was entered after trial based on the verdict of a jury.

columns.name: Agreed Judgments
columns.description: Number of cases in which the court entered a judgment based upon the mutual agreement of the parties involved in the suit.

columns.name: Bench Trials
columns.description: Number of cases in which the decision was reached after a trial or hearing by the judge, without a jury.

columns.name: All Other Cases Added
columns.description: Number of cases added to the docket in a manner other than the filing of a new, original case.

columns.name: Cases Appealed After Trial
columns.description: Number of cases for which an appeal from the final judgment of the court was filed after trial.

columns.name: Cases Appealed Without Trial
columns.description: Number of cases for which an appear from the final judgment of the court was filed without trial.

columns.name: Inactive Cases End of Month
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered at the end of the month that had been classified as inactive.

columns.name: Active Cases End of Month
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered that were classified as active and awaiting entry of a judgment at the end of the month.

columns.name: Cases Pending End of Month
columns.description: Sum of active and inactive cases at the end of the month.

columns.name: Cases Placed on Inactive Status
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered that were place in an inactive pending status because further court proceedings and activities could not continue.

columns.name: Default Judgments
columns.description: Number of cases in which the judgment was reached by default- the defendant, though served, failed to appear and answer, and judgment by default was granted in favor of the plaintiff.

columns.name: Inactive Cases Pending
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered that had been classified as inactive.

columns.name: Total Cases Disposed
columns.description: Total number of cases disposed during the month.

description: Texas Government Code §2206.155 requires the Comptroller’s office to publish a list of entities that have failed to comply with the reporting requirements of Government Code Chapter 2206, Subchapter D. The Comptroller does not make determinations regarding the accuracy or veracity of reports received under this chapter. Rather, it is incumbent upon the reporting entity to determine whether it possesses eminent domain authority such that it is required to submit a report in accordance with §2206.

description: The Recapitulation dataset displays the data of summarized and consolidated information related to a construction contract. This recapitulation includes information such as the unit of measurement, quantity of work completed, unit price, cost, and vendor information. TxDOT uses this information to determine the amount work has been completed according to the contract specifications. Recapitulation dataset includes data in previous month.

name: CPS 6.2 Average Monthly Unique Children/Young Adults in Paid Foster Care FY2016-2025

columns.name: Average Monthly Unique Children and Youth in Paid Foster Care

description: Average monthly count of unduplicated children in paid foster care by fiscal year. This dataset counts unique children regardless of payment types during the month. Calculations exclude children and young adults where cost of care was not covered by Title IV-E or state paid foster care. A young adult is any person in foster care who was 18 to 21 years of age at anytime during the fiscal year. Some children are served in more than one eligibility type in a month.

description: Before filing an informal complaint with the Public Utility Commission (PUC), customers are requested to contact their electric, telephone, water or sewer provider first and allow them to investigate the complaint. If the customer is unable to come to a satisfactory resolution with their provider, they can then file a complaint with the PUC's Consumer Protection Division (CPD). This dataset includes the complaints filed with CPD.

columns.description: THE_GEOM feature gives more information about the geometry. The geometry gives more information about the shape, size, relative position of figures, and the properties of space. All this data is stored in THE_GEOM.

columns.name: LEVEL4_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL4_SUR:  TEXT 32 Sub-Survey name of the grantee when the survey is a part of a larger refined area surveyed by a common party, and is only added if it is shown on the GLO map. A scrap file number corresponding to GLO records may also appear in the field.

columns.name: ABSTRACT_N
columns.description: ABSTRACT_N: TEXT 12 Abstract number. The Anum is comprised of the county FIPS code and the abstract number. Assigned to the surveyed parcel by the General Land Office at the time of patenting. If the abstract number field contains a "?" or is blank, then no abstract number was found.

columns.name: SCRAP_FILE
columns.description: SCRAP_FILE: TEXT 9 Scrap or mineral file number from the GLO Abstract of Land Titles.

columns.name: LEVEL1_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL1_SUR: TEXT 32 Survey name. The name of the original grantee or the name of the company, individual or eleemosynary institution that is common among a formed group of surveys as shown on the General Land Office (GLO) county patent survey map or the GLO State Abstract of Land Titles.

columns.name: LEVEL2_BLO
columns.description: LEVEL2_BLO: TEXT 10 Block Number. The number or letter used in description of a group of surveys identified as a Block on the GLO map. Example: 101

columns.name: ABSTRACT_L
columns.description: ABSTRACT_L: TEXT 11 Abstract label. Label for the abstract number.

columns.name: LEVEL3_SUR
columns.description: LEVEL3_SUR: TEXT 8 Section number. Further describes an abstracted surveyed parcel. Or, when preceded by “SUR”, a surveyed parcel further divided into numbered abstracted areas. Example: SUR 101

description: A survey is a measurement for a parcel of land. Original surveys were performed as part of the patenting process where land was transferred from the public domain. The original patented surveys are recorded at the Texas General Land Office. The construction and placement of these survey polygons are not to be used to define or establish survey boundaries. These survey polygons are provided as a public service for informational purposes only.

columns.name: OVERSIGHT DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This field indicates Federal or State oversight of Federally funded projects.

columns.name: MPO DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This field indicates if the proposed project is on a highway and location identified as a part of a Metropolitan Planning Organization (MPO) and the name of the MPO.

columns.name: LETTING STATUS DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Indicates the award status of the contract for this particular project.

columns.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CLEARANCE TYPE
columns.description: Code representing Environmental Clearance Type for a project.

columns.name: BIDDER PRE-QUALIFICATION WAIVER FLAG
columns.description: Indicates whether the vendors are required to satisfy certain pre-qualifications.

columns.name: LONGITUDE (LINE) BEGIN
columns.description: This represents longitude value of the geographical coordinate marking the begining point of a proposed project.

columns.name: LOCAL AGENCY NAME
columns.description: This indicates local agency for local agency let projects.

columns.name: LONGITUDE (LINE) END
columns.description: This represents longitude value of the geographical coordinate marking the ending point of a proposed project.

columns.name: MAXIMUM NUMBER OF WORKING DAYS
columns.description: Number of days for the contract depending on WORKING DAY TYPE.

columns.name: MOST RESTRICTIVE PROPOSED CLASS
columns.description: This field represents the proposed functional classification of the roadway section for the proposed project. This field is used to determine whether this roadway is eligible for the funds requested.

columns.name: PROJECT ACTUAL LET DATE
columns.description: Indicates the date that a CSJ went through letting or date project received construction concurrent memo.

columns.name: PROJECT CLASSIFICATION
columns.description: Indicates the type of work included in the transportation project.

columns.name: PROJECT ESTIMATE / LOW BID
columns.description: Most recent assumed cost of construction on the project.

columns.name: PROJECT ESTIMATED LET DATE
columns.description: Indicates the date that a district anticipates a project going to letting or to be obligated in the case of non-let projects.

columns.name: PROJECT ID
columns.description: Project Identification number will be assigned by TxDOTCONNECT.

columns.name: PROJECT LIMITS FROM
columns.description: Beginning location of the project.

columns.name: PROJECT NAME
columns.description: The name of the project.

columns.name: PROJECT SUB TYPE DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This is a sub classification of project types. For example, an Alternative Delivery Project may have sub types as Design Build, Design Build Finance amongst others.

columns.name: SEALED ENGINEER'S ESTIMATE TOTAL CONTRACT
columns.description: Indicates the sealed engineer's estimate for the entire contract.

columns.name: SEALED ENGINEER'S ESTIMATE PROJECT
columns.description: Indicates the sealed engineer's estimate for the project.

columns.name: SHORT DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Short description of the project.

columns.name: STATE PROJECT NUMBER
columns.description: Number assigned to the State funded projects for identification purposes to track state fundings.

columns.name: TYPE OF WORK
columns.description: Technical description of contract’s type of work to be performed on the project.

columns.name: LOCAL AGENCY EMAIL
columns.description: This indicates local agency contact person email for local agency let projects.

columns.name: LOCAL AGENCY CONTACT
columns.description: This indicates local agency contact person for local agency let projects.

columns.name: LATITUDE (LINE) END
columns.description: This represents latitude value of the geographical coordinate marking the ending point of a proposed project.

columns.name: FEDERAL PROJECT NUMBER
columns.description: Number assigned to federally funded projects for identification purposes to track federal fundings.

columns.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CLEAR TO LET DATE
columns.description: Date the Environmental Clearance Type was received on a project.

columns.name: DISTRICT/DIVISION CSTR PERFORMANCE END DATE
columns.description: Indicates when TxDOT anticipates all construction work on the project to be completed. Any work performed after the performance end date will not be eligible for federal reimbursement.

columns.name: DBE GOAL PERCENT
columns.description: Percentage of a contract's funds to be allocated to subcontractors who are designated by the department as minorities.

columns.name: CONTROLLING PROJECT ID (CCSJ)
columns.description: The primary CSJ identified as the main project for the contract.

columns.name: CONTROL SECTION JOB (CSJ)
columns.description: The key identifier representing a specific part of a highway by its defined Control Section and Job number.

columns.name: CONTROL SECTION
columns.description: The key identifier representing a specific part of a highway by its defined Control Section.

columns.name: CONTRACT LIMITS FROM
columns.description: Indicates the starting / beginning location for a proposed contract.

columns.name: CONSTRUCTION MANAGER EMAIL
columns.description: Indicates the email of the primary contact for TXDOT.

columns.name: CONSTRUCTION MANAGER
columns.description: Indicates the primary contact for TXDOT.

columns.name: CITY
columns.description: The local incorporation (City) where the project is located.

columns.name: BID RECEIVED UNTIL DATE AND TIME
columns.description: The date and time TxDOT will receive bids until.

columns.name: RIGHT OF WAY CSJ
columns.description: Specific Right-of-Way (ROW) CSJ that is associated with the construction CSJ.

columns.name: CONTRACT LIMITS TO
columns.description: Indicates the ending location for a proposed contract.

columns.name: RIGHT OF WAY PROJECT ID
columns.description: A unique identifier associated to each Right of Way project.

columns.name: PROPOSED MAIN LINE TYPE
columns.description: This reflects the type of main lanes on the highway where the proposed project location is. The Main lane types include Rural Undivided, Rural Divided, Rural Freeway, Urban Undivided, Urban Divided, and Urban Freeway.

columns.name: PROJECT STAGE
columns.description: Indicates where the project is in its lifecycle. Possible values depend upon the Project Type.

columns.name: LET TYPE DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This identifies if the project is Let by TxDOT, a Local Government, or a Non-Let.

columns.name: CONTRACT NUMBER
columns.description: Indicates the number that represents the contract being awarded.

columns.name: PROPOSAL GUARANTEE AMOUNT
columns.description: This specifies the security amount furnished with a proposal to guarantee that the bidder will put into the contract if the project is awarded.

columns.name: LATITUDE (LINE) BEGIN
columns.description: This represents latitude value of the geographical coordinate marking the beginning point of a proposed project.

columns.name: TIP YEAR
columns.description: Indicates the federal fiscal year that a project is included in the Transportation Improvement Program (TIP)/Statewide Transportation Improvement Program (STIP).

columns.name: PROPOSED MAINLANE LENGTH
columns.description: This field represents to the nearest 0.1 of a mile the number of main lane miles that currently exist on the highway location of the proposed project.

columns.name: PROJECT DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Description of the work being performed in terms that the public can understand.

columns.name: HIGHWAY
columns.description: Represents the highway type and number.

columns.name: DISTRICT/ DIVISION
columns.description: The district where the project is to be conducted. The project must be within the boundary of the district. Division has a statewide responsibility in a specialized function or asset.

columns.name: COUNTY
columns.description: The county(s) where the project location is identified. The project can be across the counties, while it is within the same control section.

columns.name: A+B ESTIMATE AMOUNT
columns.description: Indicates A+B portion of the Engineer's Estimate Amount. The amount is the Road User Cost multiplied by the Max Days.

columns.name: PROJECT LIMITS TO
columns.description: Ending location of the project.

columns.name: SEQUENCE NUMBER
columns.description: This indicates the order in which the project will be let each day.

columns.name: PROJECT STATUS
columns.description: These are predefined values associated to the project, which represent the project status. They include Active, Paused, Cancelled, Inactive and Closed.

columns.name: WORKING DAY TYPE (CCSJ)
columns.description: This specifies if it is workdays or calendar days.

columns.name: SPEC BOOK YEAR
columns.description: Indicates the year of the Spec Book. Spec Books are release every ten years.

columns.name: PROJECT TYPE
columns.description: These are Construction, Non-Let, Special, Transfer, Maintenance and Other Project types.

columns.name: PROJECT APPROVED LET DATE
columns.description: The date of Letting for the project approved on the Monthly Letting Schedule.

columns.name: Certificate of Convenience and Necessity (CCN) Number
columns.description: A CCN authorizes and requires utilities to provide continuous and adequate service to every customer who requests service in a certificated area. A CCN Number that includes a letter indicates the following: The ‘A’ is for pending registrations (i.e., a utility who has submitted an application but has not yet been granted a CCN). The ‘N’ is an exempt registration for a utility who has met specific criteria to provide retail service without having to obtain a CCN. The ‘U’ is for a tracking of utilities who we believe may be operating illegally (i.e., should have a CCN). ‘X’ is for miscellaneous/unknown reasons.

name: APC Raw July 2021 - December 2021

columns.name: current_route_id
columns.description: The 'current_route_id' is the trip route for a specified entry.

columns.name: close_date_time
columns.description: 'close_date_time' is the date/time when the door was closed. If more than one door closed at a location, then this is the latest door closing.

columns.name: bs_id
columns.description: 'bs_id' is the bus stop identification number assigned to a particular geographical bus stop within a service region. It may be matched against bs_id field in the bus_stop_data table, along with other items, to acquire detailed information about a stop.

columns.name: booking_start_date
columns.description: 'booking_start_date' is a timestamp equaling the date the booking and its associated schedules become active. The booking_num is reported by the vehicle as part of the correlation process and can be examined in the booking table to get the booking id and booking start date.

columns.name: booking_num
columns.description: 'booking_num' is a number field that uniquely identifies an imported schedule. This is reported by the vehicle as part of the correlation process, and can be examined in the booking table to get the booking id and booking start date.

columns.name: booking_id
columns.description: 'booking_id' is a short text description for the imported booking (i.e. SPR098). The booking_num is reported by the vehicle as part of the correlation process, and can be examined in the booking table to get the booking id and booking start date.

columns.name: block_id
columns.description: 'block_id' is the block identification number assigned that uniquely defines a particular route/block combination. A block usually identifies a unique schedule from other blocks upon a common primary route. Valid values for the “block_id” are between 1 and 999,999. Combined with the 'route_id' field, it is the primary key of the 'block' table.

columns.name: apc_date_time
columns.description: 'apc_date_time' is a date field describing the date on which the vehicle data was collected. The date comes from the System Date in the Data Logging files.

columns.name: actual_sequence
columns.description: 'actual_sequence' is the numeric ordering of door events/logons/trips collected in the vehicle data logging files. This is the actual sequence, not the scheduled sequence of bus stops as in the 'bus_stop_pattern' table.

columns.name: act_trip_start_time
columns.description: 'act_trip_start_time' is a timestamp that is the actual trip start time.

columns.name: tp_id
columns.description: 'tp_id' is the timepoint identification number assigned to a particular geographical timepoint within a service region. If the bus stop is also a timepoint, then the tp_id is assigned. Otherwise, it is '0'. It is defined during the schedule import process.

columns.name: non_rev_distance
columns.description: 'non_rev_distance' is the number of miles or kilometers (based on value of rep_odometer_units) on a non_revenue trip that will be traveled by the 'max_load' number of passengers after this location to the next location. Either this field or 'rev_distance' will be zero.

columns.name: vehicle_id
columns.description: 'vehicle_id' is a numeric key which identifies the vehicle that was picking up and dropping off passengers. The valid range is 1 to 65,535. This is a soft key to the 'vehicle' table.

columns.name: import_error
columns.description: 'import_error' is a software foreign key to the 'apc_error_types' table. The 'apc_error_types' table describes the types of errors, if any, detected in correlating this door event.

columns.name: quality_indicator
columns.description: 'quality_indicator' is used only if the position_source field is equal to 'GPS' or 'RSA' and is used to determine the margin of error for the GPS signal. The values are 0=GPS device has never had a valid fix; 1=Invalid data; 2=No fix; 3=2D fix (altitude not available); 4=3D fix (latitude, longitude, altitude); 5=2D fix (differentially corrected); 6=3D fix (differentially corrected).

columns.name: offs
columns.description: 'offs' is the load balanced number of passengers that alighted through all doors at this location if the balance_load flag is enabled in the apattern table. If the balance_load flag is not enabled, then the offs equals the raw_off.

columns.name: ons
columns.description: 'ons' is the load balanced number of passengers that boarded through all doors at this location if the balance_load flag is enabled in the apattern table. If the balance_load flag is not enabled, then the ons equals the raw_on.

columns.name: open_date_time
columns.description: 'open_date_time' is the date/time when the door was opened. If more than one door opened at a location, then this is the earliest door opening.

columns.name: operator_id
columns.description: 'operator_id' is the driver logged on when the APC data was collected. If the vehicle is logged off this will be zero.

columns.name: raw_max_load
columns.description: 'raw_max_load' is the current number of passengers that are on the vehicle after being adjusted by the number of passengers that boarded or alighted at this location.

columns.name: raw_off
columns.description: 'raw_off' is the number of passengers that alighted through all doors at this location. It is limited by the 'max_offs' field of the 'system_parameters' table.

columns.name: raw_on
columns.description: 'raw_on' is the number of passengers that boarded through all doors at this location. It is limited by the 'max_ons' field of the 'system_parameters' table.

columns.name: rev_distance
columns.description: 'rev_dist' is the number of miles or kilometers (based on value of rep_odometer_units) on a revenue trip that will be traveled by the 'max_load' number of passengers after this location to the next location. Either this field or ‘non_revenue_dist’ will be zero.

columns.name: rev_seconds
columns.description: 'rev_seconds' is the number of seconds on a revenue trip that it took to travel to the next location with the 'max_load' number of passengers. Either this field or 'non_rev_seconds' will be zero.

columns.name: route_id
columns.description: 'route_id' is a foreign key field to the route table. Valid “route_id” are numbers between 1 and 65,535. Combined with the 'block_id' field, it is the primary key of the 'block' table. This is a static field defined upon record creation.

columns.name: run_id
columns.description: 'run_id' is the run number that the target is logged onto. If the vehicle is logged off, this will be zero.

columns.name: sched_time
columns.description: 'sched_time' is the schedule arrival time at the timepoint if the current bus stop record is also a timepoint.

columns.name: seg_arr_time
columns.description: 'seg_arr_time' is the scheduled arrival time at the beginning of a segment.

columns.name: seg_dep_time
columns.description: 'seg_dep_time' is the scheduled departure time at the end of a segment.

columns.name: start_trip_time
columns.description: 'start_trip_time' is a timestamp that equals the scheduled time associated to the first timepoint of the trip. The date portion of the timestamp is not used.

columns.name: transit_date_time
columns.description: 'transit_date_time' is the day of the month associated to the schedule being used on the vehicle. For a given date/time, the transit day would be the same day or the day before. The transit day will be the day before in the early morning time periods.

columns.name: variation
columns.description: 'variation' is a primary key field that differentiates a route path from others on the same route and direction. Combined with the 'route_id' and 'direction_code_id' fields, it defines a unique path for route directions. It is obtained by consulting the apattern_id reported by the vehicle against the apattern table.

columns.name: veh_lat
columns.description: 'veh_lat' is the latitude reported by the vehicle. It defaults to zero.

columns.name: veh_long
columns.description: 'veh_long' is the longitude reported by the vehicle. It defaults to zero.

columns.name: position_source
columns.description: 'position_source' is used to indicate where the vehicle positioning is coming from. The values are 0=Error; 1-GPS; 2-INS device; 3=LORAN; 4=GLONASS; 5=Odometer; 6=Dead Reckoning; 7=RSA; 8=Data Not Available.

columns.name: insert_date_time
columns.description: 'insert_date_time' is the date and time of when the whole trip record is ready for insert into the database. The same timestamp is used for each trip.

columns.name: day_type_vs
columns.description: 'day_type_vs' is an identification number for both the day of the week and the schedule number for that day for vehicle schedules. It matches the id field in the day_type_vs table.

columns.name: time_id
columns.description: 'time_id' is an identifier for a specific time period during the day. A numeric identifier for time periods such as AM Peak, MidDay, PM Peak, etc.. Current project values are given in the time_period table.

columns.name: garage_id
columns.description: 'garage_id' is the division assigned to this route/block.

columns.name: import_trip_error
columns.description: 'import_trip_error' identifies the trip-level error, if any, associated with the current record.

columns.name: headsign_route
columns.description: 'headsign_route' is an identifier that defines the alternate route id in a trip. If the alternate route id is not used, then the headsign_route equals the current route id.

columns.name: ext_trip_id
columns.description: 'ext_trip_id' is a customer trip identifier. It is defined during the schedule import process.

columns.name: dwell_time
columns.description: 'dwell_time' is the number of seconds between the earliest open door and latest close door at a given location.

columns.name: direction_code_id
columns.description: 'direction_code_id' is a primary key field that identifies the route direction associated with the route path. Combined with the 'route_id' and 'variation' fields, it defines a unique path for route directions. It is obtained by consulting the apattern_id reported by the vehicle against the apattern table. It is also a software foreign key to the 'direction_codes' table.

columns.name: num_sat
columns.description: 'num_sat' indicates the number of satellites currently being used.

columns.name: non_rev_seconds
columns.description: 'non_rev_seconds' is the number of seconds on a non_revenue trip that it took to travel to the next location with the 'max_load' number of passengers. Either this field or 'rev_seconds' will be zero.

columns.name: max_load
columns.description: 'max_load' is the load balanced current number of passengers that are on the vehicle (onboard count) after being adjusted by the number of passengers that boarded or alighted at this location if the balance_load flag is enabled in the apattern table. Otherwise, the max_load equals the raw_max_load.

description: This is data represented in the Annual Enforcement Report, pursuant to Texas Water Code Section 5.126, and published each December following the end of the fiscal year. Supplemental Environmental Projects (SEPs) are environmentally beneficial projects that a respondent agrees to undertake in settlement of an enforcement action. Dollars directed to TCEQ-approved environmental projects may be used to offset assessed penalties in enforcement actions.

description: TCEQ calculates the compliance history mass classification on Sept. 1 each year, which generates compliance history ratings and classifications for all applicable entities. By Nov. 15 these ratings and classifications are then published. Customers and regulated entities are scored on their performance and assigned a corresponding rating and classification, which range from "High Performer" to "Unsatisfactory Performer". "Unclassified" indicates that there is no information on which to base a rat

name: RRC-Texas East Region Original Land Survey Polygons
description: A survey is a measurement for a parcel of land. Original surveys were performed as part of the patenting process where land was transferred from the public domain. The original patented surveys are recorded at the Texas General Land Office. The construction and placement of these survey polygons are not to be used to define or establish survey boundaries. These survey polygons are provided as a public service for informational purposes only.

name: Pre-bid Question and Answer Packet

columns.name: DISTRICT / DIVISION
columns.description: The district where the project (CSJ) is to be conducted. The project (CSJ) must be within the boundary of the district. Division has a statewide responsibility in a specialized function or asset.

columns.name: APPROVED BY
columns.description: Indicates the person who approved the answer to a prebid question.

columns.name: QUESTION RESPONSE TEXT
columns.description: Indicates the complete response to the prebid question.

columns.name: RESPONDER
columns.description: Indicates the person who provided the response to the prebid question.

columns.name: FORM SUBMITTED DATE
columns.description: Indicates the date & time of prebid Q&A Form.

columns.name: CONTROLLING PROJECT ID (CCSJ)
columns.description: The primary CSJ identify as the main project for the contract.

columns.name: FORM ID
columns.description: Indicates prebid Q&A form Identification Number.

columns.name: BID RECEIVED UNTIL DATE AND TIME
columns.description: The date & time TxDOT will receive bids until.

columns.name: RESPONSE TYPE
columns.description: Indicates the type of response.

columns.name: FORM STATUS
columns.description: Indicates the status of prebid Q&A Form.

columns.name: QUESTION TEXT
columns.description: Indicates the full description of the prebid question.

columns.name: QUESTION TITLE
columns.description: Indicates the brief description of the prebid question.

description: The Pre-bid Q&A Packet dataset displays detailed questions and answers submitted before letting, including pre-bid questions, submission date and time, responder details, and other project information. Its contents are refreshed daily with data from TxDOT’s transportation program management system, TXDOTCONNECT. The Project Information dataset includes all questions submitted by vendors and the responses provided by TxDOT.

name: CPS 4.3 Adoption - Children In Adoption Placements by Region with Demographics FY2016-2025
description: This chart counts the number of unique children in DFPS custody who lived in an adoptive placement at some point during the fiscal year and the total number of adoptive placements during the year. Children can have more than one adoptive placement. This chart includes children in DFPS custody for whom a court has appointed DFPS legal responsibility through Permanent Managing Conservatorship. columns.name: PROPOSED MAINLANE LENGTH
columns.description: This field represents to the nearest 0.1 of a mile the number of main lane miles that currently exist on the highway location of the proposed project.

columns.name: MAXIMUM NUMBER OF WORKING DAYS
columns.description: This specifies the number of days for project if it is workdays or calendar days.

columns.name: CONSTRUCTION MANAGER EMAIL
columns.description: Indicates the email of the primary contact for the Agency.

columns.name: TIP YEAR
columns.description: Indicates the federal fiscal year that a project is included in the TIP/ST

columns.name: STATE PROJECT NUMBER
columns.description: Number assigned to the project for identification purposes to track funds.

columns.name: SEALED ENGINEER'S ESTIMATE PROJECT
columns.description: Indicates the sealed engineer's estimate for the CSJ.

columns.name: PROPOSED MAIN LINE TYPE
columns.description: This reflects the type of main lanes on the highway where the proposed project location is. The Main lane types include Rural Undivided, Rural Divided, Rural Freeway, Urban Undivided, Urban Divided, and Urban Freeway.

columns.name: PROJECT SUB TYPE DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This is a sub classification of project types. For example, an Alternative Delivery Project may have sub types as Design Build, Design Build Finance amongst others.

columns.name: PROJECT NAME
columns.description: TxDOTCONNECT requires user to enter the project name for each new project to be created in this application. The Project name must be unique.

columns.name: PROJECT ID
columns.description: Project Identification number will be assigned by TxDOTCONNECT.

columns.name: PROJECT ESTIMATED LET DATE
columns.description: The date the district estimates the project will be Let.

columns.name: PROJECT DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Description of the work being performed in terms that the public can understand.

columns.name: PROJECT CLASSIFICATION
columns.description: The project classification codes are used to identify the type of work included in the transportation project.

columns.name: PROJECT APPROVED LET DATE
columns.description: The date of Letting for the project approved on the Monthly Letting Schedule.

columns.name: PROJECT ACTUAL LET DATE
columns.description: The date the project Let.

columns.name: MOST RESTRICTIVE PROPOSED CLASS
columns.description: This field represents the proposed functional classification of the roadway section for the proposed project. This field is used to determine whether this roadway is eligible for the funds requested.

columns.name: COUNTY
columns.description: The county(s) where the project location is identified. The project can be across the counties, while it is within the same control section.

columns.name: DISTRICT/ DIVISION
columns.description: The district where the project is to be conducted. The project must be within the boundary of the district. Division has a statewide responsibility in a specialized function or asset.

columns.name: DISTRICT/DIVISION CSTR PERFORMANCE END DATE
columns.description: Indicates when TxDOT anticipates all work on the project to be completed. Any work performed after the performance end date will not be eligible for federal reimbursement.

columns.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CLEARANCE TYPE
columns.description: Code representing Environmental Clearance Type for a project.

columns.name: FEDERAL PROJECT NUMBER
columns.description: Number assigned to the project for identification purposes to track funds.

columns.name: LATITUDE (LINE) BEGIN
columns.description: This represents latitude value of the geographical coordinate marking the begin point of a proposed project. The users can edit this field if they wish to provide this value as a manual input. If the user selects the begin point on the location map, then this field is automatically populated.

columns.name: PROPOSAL GUARANTEE AMOUNT
columns.description: This specifies the security amount furnished with a proposal to guarantee that the bidder will put into the contract if the project is awarded.

columns.name: A+B ESTIMATE AMOUNT
columns.description: The Total is the Road User Cost multiplied by the Max Days.

columns.name: BID RECEIVED UNTIL TIME
columns.description: The time TxDOT will receive bids until.

columns.name: BIDDER PRE-QUALIFICATION WAIVER FLAG
columns.description: This indicates if the Vendor that is interested in bidding needs to meet certain pre-qualifications.

columns.name: CONTRACT LIMITS FROM
columns.description: Indicates the starting / beginning location for a proposed project.

columns.name: CONTRACT LIMITS TO
columns.description: Indicates the ending location for a proposed project.

columns.name: CONTROL SECTION JOB (CSJ)
columns.description: The project location has to be within a pre-defined control section. If the control section is not defined and not available in the system, it needs to be requested and created outside of this application, and then made available to TxDOTCONNECT, before a new project within the control section is created.

columns.name: MAX DAY TYPE
columns.description: This specifies the number of days for project if it is workdays or calendar days.

columns.name: TYPE OF WORK
columns.description: This is the contract’s type of work. Where multiple projects are combined into one contract, this will include a summary of the type of work for all projects.

columns.name: SPEC BOOK YEAR
columns.description: Indicates the year of the Spec Book. It should default to 2014.

columns.name: SHORT DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Short description of the project.

columns.name: SEQUENCE NUMBER
columns.description: This indicates the day of letting and the order in which the project will be let each day.

columns.name: SEALED ENGINEER'S ESTIMATE TOTAL CONTRACT
columns.description: Indicates the sealed engineer's estimate for the entire contract.

columns.name: RIGHT OF WAY PROJECT ID
columns.description: A unique identifier associated to each project created in the TxDOTCONNECT application. The Project ID is auto generated by the application, when a project is successfully saved for the first time

columns.name: RIGHT OF WAY CSJ
columns.description: Specific Right-of-Way (ROW) CSJ that is associated with the construction CSJ.

columns.name: PROJECT TYPE DESCRIPTION
columns.description: These are Construction, Non-Let, Special, Transfer, Maintenance and Other Project types.

columns.name: PROJECT STATUS
columns.description: These are predefined values associated to the project, which represent the project status. They include Active, Paused, Cancelled, Inactive and Closed. These are selected through a dropdown, but the user can select from limited values available at any stage.

columns.name: PROJECT STAGE
columns.description: Indicates where the project is in its lifecycle. Possible values depend upon the Project Type.

columns.name: PROJECT ESTIMATE / LOW BID
columns.description: Most recent assumed cost of construction on the project.

columns.name: OVERSIGHT DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This field indicates Federal or State oversight of Federally funded projects.

columns.name: MPO DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This field indicates if the proposed project is on a highway and location identified as a part of a Metropolitan Planning Organization (MPO) and the name of the MPO.

columns.name: LETTING STATUS DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This identifies if the project is let by TxDOT and gives the status of the project.

columns.name: LET TYPE DESCRIPTION
columns.description: This identifies if the project is Let by TxDOT, a Local Government, or a Non-Let.

columns.name: LATITUDE (LINE) END
columns.description: This represents latitude value of the geographical coordinate marking the ending point of a proposed project. The users can edit this field if they wish to provide this value as a manual input. If the user selects the begin point on the location map, then this field is automatically populated.

columns.name: HIGHWAY
columns.description: Represents the highway type and number.

columns.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CLEAR TO LET DATE
columns.description: Date the Environmental Clearance Type was received on a project.

columns.name: CONTROLLING PROJECT ID (CCSJ)
columns.description: This is the identifier of the combined project for all associated control section jobs/project ID’s.

columns.name: CONTRACT NUMBER
columns.description: Indicates the number that represents the contract being awarded.

columns.name: CITY
columns.description: City is a legally defined government entity

columns.name: BID RECEIVED UNTIL DATE
columns.description: The date TxDOT will receive bids until.

columns.name: DBE GOAL PERCENT
columns.description: This is the percentage goal of the Disadvantaged Business Enterprise Supportive Services. The value is required on all projects where FHWA is participating in the cost of the work.

columns.name: CONSTRUCTION MANAGER
columns.description: This is the Area Engineer assigned to the project. This relates to the P5 screen in DCIS.

columns.name: WORKING DAY TYPE (CCSJ)
columns.description: This specifies the number of days for project if it is workdays or calendar days.

columns.name: CONTROL SECTION
columns.description: The project location must be within a pre-defined control section. If the control section is not defined and not available in the system, it needs to be requested and created outside of this application, and then made available to TxDOTCONNECT, before a new project within the control section is created.

columns.name: LIMITS FROM
columns.description: Beginning location of the project.

columns.name: LIMITS TO
columns.description: Ending location of the project.

columns.name: LONGITUDE (LINE) END
columns.description: This represents longitude value of the geographical coordinate marking the ending point of a proposed project. The users can edit this field if they wish to provide this value as a manual input. If the user selects the begin point on the location map, then this field is automatically populated.

description: The Archived Project Information dataset displays detailed project information such as location, type of work, and estimated cost for projects scheduled for letting within the designated two consecutive fiscal years. Its contents are refreshed hourly with data from TxDOT’s transportation program management system, TXDOTCONNECT. Archived Project Information dataset includes previous Project Information data outside of Project Information dataset.

name: Comptroller Object Numbers and Titles
description: This file comprises the list of all objects of expenditure and objects of revenue. These 4-digit codes identify specific operating statement ledger items. Objects in the 3000 series are generally revenues, while objects in the 7000 series are generally expenditures.

name: CPS 4.5 Adoptions Consummated by County FY2016-2025
description: This chart counts children who exited DFPS custody to adoption during the fiscal year. To be adopted, a court must have terminated parental rights, the child must have lived with the adoptive family for at least 6 months, the family must have been approved for adoption through a licensed child placing agency and a court must have ordered legal custody to the adoptive parents. Visit dfps.state.tx.us for information on all DFPS programs

name: APS 3.3 Investigations: Findings of Abuse By County FY2016-2025

Invalid. Based on the standard of preponderance of the evidence, it is more likely than not that the maltreatment did not occur. Unable to Determine. A preponderance of the available evidence is insufficient to support a finding of Valid or Invalid. Other. The allegation disposition Other is used when an investigation

name: CPS 4.3 Adoption Placements FY2016 - 2025
description: This chart counts the number of unique children in DFPS custody who lived in an adoptive placement at some point during the fiscal year and the total number of adoptive placements during the year. Children can have more than one adoptive placement. This chart includes children in DFPS custody for whom a court has appointed DFPS legal responsibility through Permanent Managing Conservatorship. An adoptive placement occurs when the child's caseworker, the family's case manager, and the adoptive fam

name: CPS 2.7 Children in DFPS Custody on August 31 with Permanency Goals by County FY2016-2025
provider: TX DFPS Data and Decision Support

columns.name: Children in DFPS Custody on August 31 with a Permanency Goal

description: Whenever CPS removes a child from his or her home and places the child in substitute care, staff must engage in permanency planning on behalf of the child to ensure that the child can return the child’s family if and when this can be accomplished or be placed permanently with an alternative family, preferably a kinship family, as soon as possible. Federal and state law provide only four acceptable permanency goals: Family Reunification, Adoption, Permanent Managing Conservatorship to a relative

description: If a child is suffering from a severe emotional disturbance and voluntarily relinquishing custody of the child for the sole purpose of obtaining mental health services is warranted, the Department of Family and Protective Services may be appointed managing conservator or joint managing conservator. In this case, the parent will voluntarily relinquish custody of the child to the state. This provision was added to the Texas Family Code in Fiscal Year 2014. More information at www.dfps.texas.gov

name: CPS 4.5 Adoptions Consummated by Region with Demographics FY2016-2025
description: This chart counts children who exited DFPS custody to adoption during the fiscal year. To be adopted, a court must have terminated parental rights, the child must have lived with the adoptive family for at least 6 months, the family must have been approved for adoption through a licensed child placing agency and a court must have ordered legal custody to the adoptive parents. Visit dfps.state.tx.us for information on all DFPS programs

description: This chart counts the different legal statuses granted during the fiscal year for children in DFPS custody. Children in DFPS custody are those for whom a court has appointed DFPS legal responsibility through temporary or permanent managing conservatorship or other court ordered legal basis. This chart includes any child in DFPS custody at some point during the year. Children may be duplicated in the count, if they were in and out of DFPS custody more than once during the fiscal year. description: The 83rd Legislature, 1st Called Session, enacted S.B. 4 (PLAN C235). The districts are identical to the interim plan, ordered by the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas, used in 2012 to elect members of the Texas delegation to the U.S. Congress. This plan is effective January 2013. PLAN C2100 is the representation of the current congressional districts drawn on 2020 census geography. Texas has 36 congressional districts, as determined by apportionment following the 2010 census

name: CPS 6.5 Purchased Services - Permanency Care Assistance by Funding Source FY2016-2025
description: For children who cannot reunify and for whom adoption has been ruled out, the Permanency Care Assistance (PCA) Program provides a monthly subsidy to relatives and fictive kin that take legal custody as a permanent managing conservator and have been a verified foster care provider for the child for at least 6 months. The PCA subsidy is similar to an adoption subsidy. Does not include non-recurring payments. Note: The Permanency Care Assistance program began in Fiscal Year 2011.

columns.name: Children with Disabling Conditions in Adoption Placements

description: This chart counts the number of unique children in DFPS custody who lived in an adoptive placement at some point during the fiscal year. Children in DFPS custody are those for whom a court has appointed DFPS legal responsibility through temporary or permanent managing conservatorship or other court ordered legal basis. An adoptive placement occurs when the child's caseworker, the family's case manager, and the adoptive family sign paperwork officially placing the child in the home for adoption.

name: CPS 4.4 Adoption - Disabling Conditions of Children in Adoption Placements by Region with Demographics FY2016-2025
description: This chart counts the number of unique children in DFPS custody who lived in an adoptive placement at some point during the fiscal year. Children in DFPS custody are those for whom a court has appointed DFPS legal responsibility through temporary or permanent managing conservatorship or other court ordered legal basis. An adoptive placement occurs when the child's caseworker, the family's case manager, and the adoptive family sign paperwork officially placing the child in the home for adoption.

name: CPS 6.4 Purchased Services - Adoption Subsidies by Funding Source, County, and Region FY2016-2025
description: Reflects children who receive an adoption subsidy that is paid through either federal or state funds. Subsidy includes financial payments only, not medical and non-recurring subsidies. Children are included if they received an adoption subsidy at any time during the fiscal year. This chart addresses Texas Family Code Section 264.017 subsection (b)(3).

name: CPI 5.1 Alternative Response Stages FY2016-2025
description: ABOUT THIS CHART Child Protective Investigations (CPI) conducts either a traditional investigation or Alternative Response (AR) investigation. Traditional investigations and Alternative Response investigations require caseworkers to assess safety and take needed actions to protect a child while assessing any risk of abuse or neglect in the foreseeable future. AR investigations focus on a more collaborative approach to working with families by allowing for family engagement along with other commu

columns.name: Children served in FBSS Family Preservation Services

description: Family Preservation Services (FPR) are services provided to the child and the family where the caregiver retains legal custody. This chart counts unique children served during fiscal year in Family Preservation Services (FPR) with a count of those removed. Children served in Family Preservation Services stay in their own home during services. NOTE: Family Preservation Services is under the umbrella of Family Based Safety Services (FBSS).

columns.name: Basis of Cost Determination Code
columns.description: DN# 564 - Method by which drug cost was calculated. 0 = Not Specified 1 = Average Wholesale Price (AWP) 2 = Local Wholesaler 3 = Direct 4 = Estimated Acquisition Cost 5 = Acquisition Cost 6 = Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC) 7 = Usual, Customary and Reasonable (UCR) 8 = Unit Dose 9 = Brand Medically Necessary

columns.name: Drug Name
columns.description: DN# 563 - Name of the dispensed drug or pharmaceutical.

columns.name: Dispensed as Written Code
columns.description: DN# 562 - A code denoting methodology used in dispensing the drugs or pharmaceuticals. Dispense as Written Code - ANSI DE 1329 0 = Not dispense as written 1 = Physician dispense as written 2 = Patient dispense as written 3 = Pharmacy dispense as written 4 = No generic available 5 = Brand dispensed as generic 6 = Override 7 = Substitution not allowed - brand name drug mandated by law 8 = Substitution not allowed - generic not available in marketplace 9 = Other
