description: ABOUT THIS CHART Child Protective Investigations (CPI) conducts either a traditional investigation or Alternative Response (AR) investigation. Traditional investigations and Alternative Response investigations require caseworkers to assess safety and take needed actions to protect a child while assessing any risk of abuse or neglect in the foreseeable future. AR investigations focus on a more collaborative approach to working with families by allowing for family engagement along with other commu

name: CPS 2.6 Conservatorship - Children With Legal Statuses Granted During Year by Region With Demographics FY2016-2025
description: This chart counts the number of unique children for whom a court entered a legal status order during the fiscal year. If a court entered multiple different legal statuses for a child during the fiscal year, the child is only counted once. A description of the different types of legal statuses is in the CPS glossary. Visit dfps.texas.gov for information on substitute care placements and all DFPS programs.

name: CPS 5.2 Circles of Support FY2016-2025
description: Based on the Family Group Decision Making (FGDM) philosophy, Circles of Support (COS) are meetings that focus on the youth and their plans for transitioning to a successful adulthood. They are led by the youth whenever possible. Participants represent a broad spectrum of the youth’s support network. The youth choses who they want to include in their COS: Participants can be the youth’s siblings, birth family members, foster or kinship care providers, teachers, relatives, church members, friends,

name: CPS 3.5 1st Children in Substitute Care First Placement Type after Removal FY2016-2025
description: A removal occurs when CPS determines that a child cannot safely remain in their own home and DFPS needs to seek legal custody to ensure child safety. Removals can occur in an investigation, family preservation, family substitute care or family reunification stage. For removals at the end of the Fiscal Year, the placement represented may be from the following Fiscal Year.

columns.name: HQ City
columns.description: Type A EDCs are generally located in counties with less than 500,000 people, or in cities located within or adjacent to Bexar, Dallas, El Paso, Harris, Hidalgo, Tarrant, or Travis counties. Type B EDCs can be formed anywhere in Texas, regardless of the community’s size and location. Type B EDCs located within communities of less than 20,000 people enjoy a wide-range of allowable expenditures. These corporations can fund land, buildings, equipment, facilities, expenditures, targeted infrastructure, and improvements found by the board of directors to promote new or expanded business development (with no “primary job” requirement). Any project under $10,000 for a Type B EDC of this size does not require approval by the city council or governing authority. Any project over $10,000 requires a resolution and at least two separate readings by the city council

description: This chart counts the different legal statuses granted during the fiscal year for children in DFPS custody. Children in DFPS custody are those for whom a court has appointed DFPS legal responsibility through temporary or permanent managing conservatorship or other court ordered legal basis. Summer meal programs operate mid-May through the end of August. An overview of all summer meal program data available on the Texas Open Data Portal can be found at our TDA Data Overview - Summer Meal Programs page.

name: CPS 3.4 Children in Substitute Care on August 31 - Sibling Groups Placed Together FY2016-2025
description: Divides sibling groups into two groups: Those sibling groups where all siblings are in the same placement on August 31 and those who are not all together with an additional filter for sibling group size. In this context, siblings are defined as children in substitute care who were removed from the same household whether in the same, or separate, removal events.

description: ABOUT THIS CHART Child Protective Investigations (CPI) conducts either a traditional investigation or Alternative Response (AR) investigation. Traditional investigations and Alternative Response investigations require caseworkers to assess safety and take needed actions to protect a child while assessing any risk of abuse or neglect in the foreseeable future. AR investigations focus on a more collaborative approach to working with families by allowing for family engagement along with other commu

name: CPI 3.9 Abuse/Neglect Investigations and Alternative Response: Alleged and Confirmed Victim Recidivism FY2016-FY2025
description: Recidivism is calculated as a percentage of all alleged victims and child principals in alternative response and investigation stages, who did not receive any ongoing services, who had a subsequent confirmed allegation, or case open for ongoing services within 12 months. columns.name: MealServiceType
columns.description: Describes method of meal service. Congregate meal service indicates meals are consumed on-site. Beginning 2023, USDA allowed approved rural sites to provide non-congregate meal service (meals consumed off-site). Hybrid meal service indicates site was approved to serve both congregate and non-congregate meals. Displayed as: Congregate, Non-congregate - [type of non-congregate meal delivery], Hybrid - [type of non-congregate meal delivery] .

description: About the Agency The Texas Department of Agriculture administers 12 U.S. Department of Agriculture nutrition programs in Texas including the National School Lunch and School Breakfast Programs and the Child and Adult Care Food Program (CACFP). TDA’s Food and Nutrition division provides technical assistance and training resources to partners operating the programs and oversees the USDA reimbursements they receive to cover part of the cost associated with serving food in their facilities. name: Digital Transformation IRDR Data
description: The Texas Department of Information Resources (DIR) has established a digital transformation guide to assist agencies with modernizing agency operations and services with respect to electronic data and converting agency information into electronic data. The purpose of the guide is to help Texas government take the next steps to advance digital transformation and improve the customer experience, no matter where the organization is on its digital journey. columns.name: Release of Information Code
columns.description: DN# 526 - Identifies whether the release of information related to a claim is authorized or not. A = Appropriate release of information on file at health care service provider or at a URA. I = Informed consent to release medical information for conditions or diagnosis regulated by Federal Statues. M = The provider has a limited or restricted ability to release data related to a claim. N = No, provider is not allowed to release data. O = On file at payor or at plan sponsor. Y = Yes, provider has signed statement permitting release of medical billing data related to the claim.

description: The Texas Department of Insurance, Division of Workers’ Compensation (DWC) maintains a database of pharmacy medical billing services (SV4). It contains charges, payments, and prescriptions billed on a DWC Form-066, Statement of Pharmacy Services by pharmacies with dates of service more than five years old going back to 2010. For datasets from the past five years, see pharmacy medical billing services (SV4) header information. The header identifies insurance carriers, injured employees, employers

name: Landlord/Tenant Caseload CY 2018-2022

columns.name: Inactive Cases End of Month
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered at the end of the month that had been classified as inactive.

columns.name: Cases Appealed Without Trial
columns.description: Number of cases for which an appear from the final judgment of the court was filed without trial.

columns.name: Agreed Judgments
columns.description: Number of cases in which the court entered a judgment based upon the mutual agreement of the parties involved in the suit.

columns.name: Default Judgments
columns.description: Number of cases in which the judgment was reached by default- the defendant, though served, failed to appear and answer, and judgment by default was granted in favor of the plaintiff.

columns.name: All Other Cases Added
columns.description: Number of cases added to the docket in a manner other than the filing of a new, original case.

columns.name: Cases Reactivated
columns.description: Number of cases that had previously been placed in an inactive pending status, bus for which further court proceedings and activities can now be resumed so that the case can proceed to disposition.

columns.name: New Cases Filed
columns.description: Total number of new cases filed during the month.

columns.name: Inactive Cases Pending
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered that had been classified as inactive.

columns.name: Active Cases Pending
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered that were active at the beginning of the month.

columns.name: Active Cases End of Month
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered that were classified as active and awaiting entry of a judgment at the end of the month.

columns.name: All Other Dispositions
columns.description: Number of dispositions not clearly identifiable as any of the above categories.

columns.name: Cases Pending
columns.description: All cases previously filed in which a judgment had not been entered at the beginning of the month.

columns.name: Bench Trials
columns.description: Number of cases in which the decision was reached after a trial or hearing by the judge, without a jury.

columns.name: Jury Trials
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment was entered after trial based on the verdict of a jury.

columns.name: Cases Dismissed for Want of Prosecution
columns.description: Number of cases dismissed because the plaintiff or petitioner did not appear or otherwise made no effort to pursue their case.

columns.name: Cases Non-Suited or Dismissed by Plaintif
columns.description: Number of cases in which a dismissal was entered at the request of the plaintiff or petitioner or dismissed by agreement of both parties.

columns.name: Total Cases Disposed
columns.description: Total number of cases disposed during the month.

columns.name: Cases Placed on Inactive Status
columns.description: Number of cases in which a judgment had not been entered that were place in an inactive pending status because further court proceedings and activities could not continue.

columns.name: Cases Pending End of Month
columns.description: Sum of active and inactive cases at the end of the month.

columns.name: Cases Appealed After Trial
columns.description: Number of cases for which an appeal from the final judgment of the court was filed after trial.

description: The dataset contains annual Landlord/Tenant caseload information by court from 2018-2022. Landlord/Tenant cases include: 1) Eviction- All suits for eviction (recovery of possession of premises) brought to recover possession of real property under Chapter 24 of the Texas Property Code, often by a landlord against a tenant. description: The 83rd Legislature, 1st Called Session, enacted S.B. 3 (PLAN H358), the plan used to elect members to the Texas House from 2014-2018. The U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas ordered changes to districts 90, 97 and 99 in Tarrant County for the 2020 elections. The resulting statewide plan (PLAN H414) is effective January 2021. All data is based on the 2010 census. For more information, visit: https://redistricting.capitol.texas.gov/Current-districts#st-house-section.

name: CPI 3.12 Family Team Meetings FY2016-2025
provider: TX DFPS Data and Decision Support
description: A Family Team Meeting (FTM) is a family-centered rapid response meeting CPI uses to try and prevent a removal by engaging caregivers, parents and extended family and friends to address child safety concerns. An FTM is not limited to an investigation and can occur at any point or stage in which CPI or CPS is involved with a family. FOOTNOTES Race/Ethnicity data are that of the primary caregiver. More information at www.dfps.texas.gov

description: This project is part of a larger plan to transfer commonly requested TJJD data onto the Texas Open Data Portal. This will allow for greater efficiency in sharing publicly available information and answering Public Information Requests (PIRs). This dataset provides an overview of TJJD’s average daily population (ADP) by location type since FY 1980. Location types include State-Operated Secure, Halfway Houses, Contract Care, and Parole.

name: Texas Commission on Environmental Quality - Remediation Division

columns.name: County
columns.description: The county that the site is located in.

columns.name: Received/Sent Date
columns.description: The date that the document was received or sent by the TCEQ Remediation program. The received date and document date may differ for incoming documents. Outgoing documents have the same received and document date.

columns.name: Direction
columns.description: Direction indicates if the document is being received by the TCEQ Remediation Program or being sent to the applicant/responsible party. Incoming is being sent to the TCEQ by the applicant/responsible party. Outgoing is being sent to the applicant/responsible party by the TCEQ.

columns.name: Communication Type
columns.description: Description of what the document pertains to. Please refer to the TCEQ Remediation Program Communication Types list for the plain language names of communication types by program here.

columns.name: Site Name
columns.description: The name of the site or facility based on the description that the applicant/responsible party provided to TCEQ Remediation Program at the time the site came into its respective program, and/or the name the TCEQ assigned the site upon accepting it into a Remediation Program.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: The physical address of the site, which is usually verified with the USPS by the applicant/responsible party or the TCEQ Remediation Program. It typically includes one street number and a street name. If this field is blank, please refer to the Address Description field.

columns.name: Address Description
columns.description: The address description provides additional details about the location of the site. This could be multiple street numbers for larger sites with multiple parcels. It could also be a description of the intersections of where the site is located. If this field is blank, please refer to the Address field.

columns.name: Communication ID
columns.description: The number used to track the specific incoming or outgoing document submitted to or generated by the TCEQ Remediation Program. A communication ID is generated for each incoming and outgoing document. Also known as Comm ID and Tracking Number.

columns.name: Document Date
columns.description: The date the document was generated (i.e. the date on the document) by the applicant/responsible party or the TCEQ Remediation Program.

columns.name: Site ID
columns.description: The site identification number is issued by a TCEQ Remediation Program and noted on the TCEQ outgoing correspondence.

columns.name: Program
columns.description: The TCEQ Remediation Program abbreviation (ex: VCP, IOP, IHWCA, MSD).

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city the site is located in. If the site is not within a city limit, the nearest city will be listed.

description: The search portal displays the last five years of incoming and outgoing documents receipt/submittal data for the following TCEQ Office of Waste, Remediation Division programs: Industrial Hazardous Waste Corrective Action (IHWCA), Innocent Owner/Operator Program (IOP), Leaking Petroleum Storage Tanks (LPST), Municipal Setting Designation (MSD), and Voluntary Cleanup Program (VCP).

description: This dashboard tracks six month and one-year outcomes for children who were in an investigation that was Administratively Closed and subsequently alleged as a victim in a new investigation. name: TCA Designated Cultural Districts FY21
description: As authorized by H.B. 2208 of the 79th Legislature, the Texas Commission on the Arts (TCA) can designate cultural districts in cities across Texas. Cultural districts are special zones that harness the power of cultural resources to stimulate economic development and community revitalization. These districts can become focal points for generating businesses, attracting tourists, stimulating cultural development and fostering civic pride. This is the current list of designated cultural districts

name: TCA Designated State Artists
description: The Texas State Legislature designates Texas State Artists during each Legislative Session. Texas Commission on the Arts (TCA) manages the Texas State Artists nomination process. These positions, including state poet laureate, state musician, state two-dimensional artist and state three-dimensional artist, are one-year appointments. Appointees are selected for their years of excellence and dedicated commitment to the arts in Texas. Texas State Artists represent the best of Texas’ rich and divers

name: Local Hotel Occupancy Tax Reporting 2025
description: In the early 1970s, the Texas Legislature authorized certain local governments to begin collecting a hotel occupancy tax (HOT). Almost two decades later, the Legislature offered hotel occupancy taxing authority as one of several revenue options to support sports and community venues. The tax may be levied by a city, county or a partnership between the two. description: This dataset lists site information, waste codes, and waste descriptions for episodic notifications for unregistered generators. Episodic notifications for unregistered generators are temporary waste generation events that require notification but do not require a solid waste registration. Episodic notifications prior to February 7, 2022 were referred to as one-time shipments.

description: Texas State Expenditures by County shows where state dollars are spent. It lists state expenditures by agency and type of expenditure in each county. description: Texas State Expenditures by County shows where state dollars are spent. It lists state expenditures by agency and type of expenditure in each county. This report was created using information from the state's Uniform Statewide Accounting System. It reflects actual net expenditures of funds from accounts held in the State Treasury during the state's fiscal year, Sept. 1 through Aug. 31.
